page: Microsoft Azure D8s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities for remote disk operations.

Performance benchmarks place this server consistently in the average tier across most workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1627.0 and a multi-core score of 6064.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 11399.88. Memory bandwidth is measured at 415.44 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 55.69 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. While single-threaded gzip compression performs at 28.7 GB/sec, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression represents a performance bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 32.2 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the D8s_v5 offers standard cost efficiency for general-purpose workloads by balancing compute and memory without the added cost of bundled local storage or specialized GPU hardware. It is suitable for web serving, database operations, and software compilation, as evidenced by its static web serving throughput of 11.46 GB/sec and Redis SET performance of 7.85 million operations per second.
description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It provides 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on Premium Storage for remote disk access. Benchmark results indicate average performance across most metrics, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 6064.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.85 million operations per second. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25% at 32.2 GB/sec. This server is configured for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage support. It delivers balanced average performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8s_v5 is an 8-vCPU general-purpose virtual machine with 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and memory-to-core ratios optimized for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or integrated GPUs
Average performance across standard CPU, memory, and database benchmarks
Bottlenecked multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance in the bottom 25%
Suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and database operations
